channel.title: Comments for page "15. Ms. Sweetie"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "15. Ms. Sweetie"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The insufferable characterization. I did want to like this but after the 'penis' line, I just rolled my eyes. Sort of echoing the post above (Dearheart's) because this is a cliché portrayal of "'subverted' damsel-in-distress, strong female character." Upsetting particularly because I was on a binge through this collaborative and all this build-up for this one character, and it just feels incredibly wasted. I think if not for that, with the stuff especially about the changing memories, I would've enjoyed this.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: InsanitysBane, please review criticism policy. In particular, how you're expected to be constructive when giving feedback. We ask this because what you're saying here does not help the author improve their work and is no more informative than a downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What made it so hard for you? Not the author, just curious as someone who enjoyed this article. Tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well this was unbearable to read through.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And perhaps I should change the negative parts lest it come off as an endorsement, but…

Bast, no.
It's another thing I have countered Dearheart with in my post above. One of the more insipid things you can see in fiction is disallowing characters (specifically, protagonists, or ones on their side) to be wrong about things (which of course usually translates to them not countering the author's opinions in any way since nobody knowingly holds to a wrong opinion, but whatever), which murders both characterization and realism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My interpretation agrees with this; presuming the existence of an Isabel Wondertainment, she's unlikely to have made these particular Little Misters at least. I'd assumed a board of directors myself; a predecessor Wondertainment would work too.

I find it interesting how Ms. Sweetie thinks the one person behind the Wondertainment name has to have a penis

I intended her to be totally wrong there, because everything she's experienced says she has more than one creator, and it would be silly to imagine just one person was responsible for creating her. Likely most of them were dudes, but they weren't thinking of her as a person (even though she IS a person) any more than they think of the other Misters as a person (even though they all — or almost all — are people).

It's very likely some of those did try to make her stupider for sexist reasons; but some of the people involved with that likely had other motives, like trying to make her personality more appropriate, or just trying to make her less dangerous considering her superpower (is scary as fuck). Ultimately, this is supposed to be left up in the air.

But, I mean, just look at Mr. Clumsy. There's absolutely no chance he started out that accident-prone, even when you account for the supernatural add-ons to force accidents to happen when he won't cause them on his own. Mr. Mad gets waaaaay too angry too often to have begun that way; he has also been mentally altered. Mr. Mission has had a total personality overhaul. There's been some pretty hefty work done on these people. Pretty hefty indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
, gender exploited for *ear-piercing screech*.

This is pretty fucking goofy feedback, though. And an anti-feminist cliche; anything women regarding women's rights (or even saying anything beyond stereotypical lady shit) is regarded as 'shrill' and 'screechy'. Bravo.

Why could this character not just be a character that was female?

Have you paid literally any attention at all? :P Her entire concept is "the girl one". She's the manufactured Smurfette. Every other Mister (with the notable exception of Redd) has some sort of Theme beyond their gender. But this character is literally called "Ms. Sweetie". The entire point is for her to be exaggeratedly female, not even necessarily realistically whatsoever.

As for psuedo-feminist cliches, yes, there are a couple there. "If there's a single person behind the Dr. Wondertainment moniker, you can bet that person has a penis" in particular is not something an actual decent feminist would say for any number of reasons, not least because of the transphobic equation of penises with men with male behavior.

The reason for this is — well, VAE is actually pretty on the money there. Living the way she does has got to be pretty freaky, and she has some barely-suppressed issues with even identifying as a woman. She's been forced into a gross Smurfette parody, probably on purpose, and almost certainly not directly for reasons of misogyny (though that likely had something to do with it indirectly); Wondertainment patterned the Misters after 80's-90's children's toys. Them's the rules: a shitload of dudes and one lady who has to fill all the lady stereotypes. They probably didn't intend for that to cause her to act a bit more adult than the rest of them, nor for the other Little Misters to treat her the way they do, but it's an unsurprising result.

But, I mean, the psuedo-feminist cliche stuff is a rather small part of this piece. If you dislike it on those counts, very well, but it's a bit odd to claim it's 'every cliche in the book'. That just makes me think you're not particularly familiar with the book.

(Or, perhaps you're including the parts where she's full-on feminist, IE where she tries to embrace her own agency and refuses to play the victim role. In which case, I simply disagree.)

And perhaps I should change the negative parts lest it come off as an endorsement, but… I also don't want to make her a perfect rainbow of a person. Seems a bit against the character concept. Though given that I'm genderqueer, maybe the transphobic stuff in there is particularly a bad idea, but… eh, I dunno. I'll have to think about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would almost agree, but ultimately, why can't there be a character that thinks in such a way? Especially given the abuse she's endured either way (and from men to boot) , I'm not the least surprised Ms. Sweetie would think in these terms.

Besides, this tidbit made me keep my upvote from who knows when.

What was the name of that old poem? From that collection they gave me when they were trying to make me properly feminine? Ah, yes. "Vengeance is sweet."

See, it even fits my theme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now that I've come back across this, I see that it went from 'rape = a character and a story', to a one-dimensional mouthpiece for every pseudo-feminist cliche in the book.

Why could this character not just be a character that was female? Gender exploited for rape drama, gender exploited for *ear-piercing screech*. Either way, exploited. From the first iteration to this one, it's just been so…used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can safely say that Isabel is not the Wondertainment who made the Misters. She's far too innocent of mind for that. But, there are/have been a great many other Wondertainments, and Wondertainment has always been a sort of trickster god. One of them might have done it.

Alternatively, the Little Misters carry the branding, but weren't actually designed by Wondertainment.

Of course this is just my 2 cents, applicable only towards my interpretation of Wondertainment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find it interesting how Ms. Sweetie thinks the one person behind the Wondertainment name has to have a penis, and the most defining piece of characterization for Dr. Wondertainment is of "Isabel Helga Anastasia Parvati Wondertainment V, PhD."

I know, I know, there is no canon, but the idea of that particular Dr. Wondertainment trying to make Ms. Sweetie stupider is very revealing. Maybe the board of directors is primarily male, or maybe she's just that distant from reality.

Either way, I like the series as a whole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The improvements work. Have this upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did some (more) badly needed polishing. Toned down some of the over the top stuff and some of the cliche female in distress tropes. Also took out the torture porn implication — overdone.

This has always been one of my weakest pieces, and I think it's because it was originally based on some personal fears (rape, gender stuff) that I in practice played out in really generic ways; the whole thing had/has a bunch of generic elements. Hopefully the edits help a little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True. By the way, where did the term scip come from? I know what it means but who made it up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't have to be an existing scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What SCP is she referring to when she said, "she's all about the dick."?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that you edited it. I'm new here, but what it is NOW versus what I see it used to be… I greatly prefer this version.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But yes, not liking the development of that character so much. Abstaining from vote at the moment.

How do you mean? I'm the author and may be willing to tweak things. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I actually like the character here. She's certainly cynical about being the manufacured "sweetheart", but at the same time still keeps part of it in her, whether or not she'd admit to believing that. She's "mature", though only like a young adult looking back at a blissful childhood with scorn. At least she knows that she doesn't know all the answers.
But yes, not liking the development of that character so much. Abstaining from vote at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've edited. Not a total overhaul, but I removed the rape altogether and added some hints as to her previous life.

Perhaps I could have rewritten to include the rape in a less ham-handed way, but this comment -

Yeah, I'm getting kind of weary of the rape-as-character-development story or the woman-created-to-be-sex-object story.

- struck home enough that I realized I could probably do better by taking a different tack. Because Sorts is right, and I've seen it myself a bit too much, so I have no business using it unless I have a truly unique spin on it - and I don't, at least for this story. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mr. Redd

is mine.

Don't care that it's not actually a pun, I cannot read that without thinking of Horatio Caine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For what its worth i never got the sense that she enjoyed any of it at all

The way she was describing it sounded like she was avoiding a traumatic event, I mean, she's not even attracted to guys and she is trying to find and kill redd now

I think the strong point of this one to me is that she's cynical about her ability and uses it to get information about redd, yet is still empathetic towards other people!

but that is because im a nerrrrrd

Oh edit though, so that said, i kinda agree with sorts' comment down there though i think it really does have potential

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Huh.

I kinda like this idea.

Hmmmmmm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, that's an idea.

She comments that it would have been easier if it was rape. Rape, she could have dealt with.

… at least her eyes grew back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I'm getting kind of weary of the rape-as-character-development story or the woman-created-to-be-sex-object story. I didn't hate this, but man it would be better if it was something else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Get rid of her comment about tits. Maybe she didn't come with this figure.

There's potential here. There's much potential here (and that's why I upvoted). There're even some good parts.

But yoric's right, this is flawed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

:|

Anyway. I do not want this to read as titillating. The character certainly doesn't find it titillating, so if it comes off that way, I probably wrote it badly.

As for being hamhanded… Ugh. Now that you point it out, and now that I've re-read specifically looking for that, I see what you're talking about. So I'm going to clean this up.

So, anyone who agrees or disagrees with yoric's critique - particularly you people who have upvoted this to where it stands currently at +11 - and wants input in said cleanup (for instance, that I should or shouldn't change something in particular), leave a comment here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was supposed to be hamhanded, but actually…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cut a couple of those lines because I agree they're a bit too over the top.

As for the rest, I'll look again later. I appreciate the feedback.

(Though, one-handed? Jesus Christ, yoric o_O)

For the record, I actually was attempting to imply disassociation with the rape, not enjoyment. If it didn't work, there you go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is… Well.

Awful, in the way that only a piece of fiction themed around a female character implied to enjoy being raped that was written entirely by men can be. You've pretty much taken a steaming dump on subtlety and characterization with this one, and I'm heavily disappointed.

I'm not so sure I "came with" these double-Ds, if you know what I mean.

Who needs to act sweet when you have a nature like mine?

I don't need to sugarcoat or forget. I keep my distance from the emotions, sure, but I remember all of it. I remember how he raped me over and over again, and how when he got tired of my natural orifices he cut new ones. I remember how he'd cut off my fingers and toes and then sew them back on, and how he moved up to my other body parts when he got bored with those. What he did with my eyes and my lips…

Yeah. Way to convincingly write a deep and interesting character, there, guys.

Downvoted. If I wanted to read bad one handed fanfic I wouldn't be on this wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In that case… Not voting. But I like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As we near the end of this sequence, here's Ms. Sweetie, my second direct contribution to the series.

Important Note: Credit to TroyL for the original idea. The central idea - what happened between Ms. Sweetie and Mr. Redd - is all his. (And that includes the use of that idea in the other Little Mister series, so it's important to point out!)

The writing, characterization, and the implied Xanatos Gambit, are mine. Any problems and issues and any bad writing are also all mine.

As always, be brutal, and be sure to tell me if you like it, too.
